        'target_draw_session' => "ALTER TABLE `{$rawName}` ADD COLUMN `target_draw_session` varchar(40) NOT NULL DEFAULT '' AFTER `target_draw_at`",
        'target_timezone_id' => "ALTER TABLE `{$rawName}` ADD COLUMN `target_timezone_id` varchar(64) NOT NULL DEFAULT '' AFTER `target_draw_session`",
        'target_prediction_family' => "ALTER TABLE `{$rawName}` ADD COLUMN `target_prediction_family` varchar(30) NOT NULL DEFAULT '' AFTER `target_timezone_id`",
        'target_prediction_type' => "ALTER TABLE `{$rawName}` ADD COLUMN `target_prediction_type` varchar(50) NOT NULL DEFAULT '' AFTER `target_prediction_family`"
    );

    foreach ($requiredCols as $colName => $alterSql) {
        try {
            $db->setQuery(
                "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM INFORMATION_SCHEMA.COLUMNS"
              . " WHERE TABLE_SCHEMA = DATABASE()"
              . "   AND TABLE_NAME = " . $db->quote($rawName)
              . "   AND COLUMN_NAME = " . $db->quote($colName)
            );
            if ((int)$db->loadResult() <= 0) {
                $db->setQuery($alterSql);
                $db->execute();
            }
        } catch (Exception $e) {
        }
    }

    $requiredIndexes = array(
        'idx_run_identity_key' => "ALTER TABLE `{$rawName}` ADD KEY `idx_run_identity_key` (`run_identity_key`)",
        'idx_prediction_target_id' => "ALTER TABLE `{$rawName}` ADD KEY `idx_prediction_target_id` (`prediction_target_id`)",
        'idx_target_game_draw' => "ALTER TABLE `{$rawName}` ADD KEY `idx_target_game_draw` (`target_game_id`,`target_draw_date`)",
        'uq_user_lineage_draw_source' => "ALTER TABLE `{$rawName}` ADD UNIQUE KEY `uq_user_lineage_draw_source` (`user_id`,`run_identity_key`,`draw_date`,`source`)"
    );

    foreach ($requiredIndexes as $indexName => $alterSql) {
        try {
            $db->setQuery(
                "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM INFORMATION_SCHEMA.STATISTICS"
              . " WHERE TABLE_SCHEMA = DATABASE()"
              . "   AND TABLE_NAME = " . $db->quote($rawName)
              . "   AND INDEX_NAME = " . $db->quote($indexName)
            );
            if ((int)$db->loadResult() <= 0) {
                $db->setQuery($alterSql);
                $db->execute();
            }
        } catch (Exception $e) {
        }
    }

    return true;
}

/**
 * Insert or update a performance record.
 * Uses UNIQUE KEY (user_id, run_id, draw_date, source) to prevent duplicates.
 * Including draw_date in the key means the same run can be scored once per draw
 * date - ensuring true historical accumulation across multiple draw cycles.
 *
 * @param  object $db
 * @param  int    $userId
 * @param  array  $data
 * @return bool
 */
function skaiUpsertPerformanceRecord($db, $userId, array $data)
{
    $userId = (int) $userId;
    if ($userId <= 0) {
        return false;
    }

    $prefix  = $db->getPrefix();
    $tblName = '`' . $prefix . 'skai_learning_performance`';
    $now     = date('Y-m-d H:i:s');

    $actualJson  = json_encode(array_values((array)($data['actual_numbers'] ?? array())), JSON_UNESCAPED_UNICODE);    $rankingJson = json_encode(array_values((array)($data['predicted_ranking'] ?? array())), JSON_UNESCAPED_UNICODE);
    $comboJson   = json_encode((array)($data['combo_hit_summary'] ?? array()), JSON_UNESCAPED_UNICODE);
    $notesJson   = json_encode((array)($data['notes'] ?? array()), JSON_UNESCAPED_UNICODE);

    $avgRank    = (isset($data['avg_winning_rank'])    && $data['avg_winning_rank']    !== null) ? round((float)$data['avg_winning_rank'],    2) : null;
    $bestRank   = (isset($data['best_winning_rank'])   && $data['best_winning_rank']   !== null) ? (int)$data['best_winning_rank']             : null;
    $worstRank  = (isset($data['worst_winning_rank'])  && $data['worst_winning_rank']  !== null) ? (int)$data['worst_winning_rank']            : null;
    $medianRank = (isset($data['median_winning_rank']) && $data['median_winning_rank'] !== null) ? round((float)$data['median_winning_rank'], 2) : null;
    $wScore     = (isset($data['weighted_quality_score']) && $data['weighted_quality_score'] !== null) ? round((float)$data['weighted_quality_score'], 6) : null;

    $runDateSql         = !empty($data['run_date'])  ? $db->quote((string)$data['run_date'])  : 'NULL';
    $drawDateSql        = !empty($data['draw_date']) ? $db->quote((string)$data['draw_date']) : 'NULL';
    $targetDrawDateSql  = !empty($data['target_draw_date']) ? $db->quote((string)$data['target_draw_date']) : 'NULL';
    $targetDrawAtSql    = !empty($data['target_draw_at']) ? $db->quote((string)$data['target_draw_at']) : 'NULL';
    $avgSql             = ($avgRank    !== null) ? $avgRank    : 'NULL';
    $bestSql            = ($bestRank   !== null) ? $bestRank   : 'NULL';
    $worstSql           = ($worstRank  !== null) ? $worstRank  : 'NULL';
    $medianSql          = ($medianRank !== null) ? $medianRank : 'NULL';
    $wSql               = ($wScore     !== null) ? $wScore     : 'NULL';

    $runUuid             = preg_replace('/[^a-zA-Z0-9\-_.]/', '', (string)($data['run_uuid'] ?? ''));
    $snapshotRunUuid     = preg_replace('/[^a-zA-Z0-9\-_.]/', '', (string)($data['snapshot_run_uuid'] ?? ''));
    $runIdentityKey      = (string)($data['run_identity_key'] ?? '');
    $predictionTargetId  = preg_replace('/[^a-zA-Z0-9\-_.]/', '', (string)($data['prediction_target_id'] ?? ''));
    $predictionTargetKey = (string)($data['prediction_target_key'] ?? '');
    $targetGameId        = (string)($data['target_game_id'] ?? '');
    $targetLotteryId     = (int)($data['target_lottery_id'] ?? 0);
    $targetDrawSession   = (string)($data['target_draw_session'] ?? '');
    $targetTimezoneId    = (string)($data['target_timezone_id'] ?? '');
    $targetPredFamily    = (string)($data['target_prediction_family'] ?? '');
    $targetPredType      = (string)($data['target_prediction_type'] ?? '');

    $sql = "INSERT INTO {$tblName}
        (`user_id`,`lottery_id`,`lottery_name`,`draw_date`,`run_date`,`run_id`,`run_uuid`,`snapshot_run_uuid`,`run_identity_key`,
         `prediction_target_id`,`prediction_target_key`,`target_game_id`,`target_lottery_id`,`target_draw_date`,`target_draw_at`,`target_draw_session`,`target_timezone_id`,`target_prediction_family`,`target_prediction_type`,`source`,
         `actual_numbers_json`,`predicted_ranking_json`,
         `avg_winning_rank`,`best_winning_rank`,`worst_winning_rank`,`median_winning_rank`,`weighted_quality_score`,
         `top10_hits`,`top20_hits`,`top30_hits`,`top50_hits`,`missing_hits_count`,`combo_hit_summary_json`,
         `tail_enabled`,`tail_active`,`tail_promoted_count`,`tail_promoted_hit_count`,
         `calibration_enabled`,`calibration_active`,
         `shared_learning_enabled`,`pairwise_enabled`,`gibbs_enabled`,`live_tuning_enabled`,
         `confidence_level`,`trend_bucket`,`notes_json`,`created_at`,`updated_at`)
        VALUES (
         " . (int)$userId . ",
         " . (int)($data['lottery_id'] ?? 0) . ",
         " . $db->quote((string)($data['lottery_name'] ?? '')) . ",
         {$drawDateSql},
         {$runDateSql},
         " . (int)($data['run_id'] ?? 0) . ",
         " . $db->quote($runUuid) . ",
         " . $db->quote($snapshotRunUuid) . ",
         " . $db->quote($runIdentityKey) . ",
         " . $db->quote($predictionTargetId) . ",
         " . $db->quote($predictionTargetKey) . ",
         " . $db->quote($targetGameId) . ",
         " . $targetLotteryId . ",
         {$targetDrawDateSql},
         {$targetDrawAtSql},
         " . $db->quote($targetDrawSession) . ",
         " . $db->quote($targetTimezoneId) . ",
         " . $db->quote($targetPredFamily) . ",
         " . $db->quote($targetPredType) . ",
         " . $db->quote((string)($data['source'] ?? '')) . ",
         " . $db->quote((string)$actualJson) . ",
         " . $db->quote((string)$rankingJson) . ",
         {$avgSql},{$bestSql},{$worstSql},{$medianSql},{$wSql},
         " . (int)($data['top10_hits'] ?? 0) . ",
         " . (int)($data['top20_hits'] ?? 0) . ",
         " . (int)($data['top30_hits'] ?? 0) . ",
         " . (int)($data['top50_hits'] ?? 0) . ",
         " . (int)($data['missing_hits_count'] ?? 0) . ",
         " . $db->quote((string)$comboJson) . ",
         " . (int)($data['tail_enabled'] ?? 0) . ",
         " . (int)($data['tail_active'] ?? 0) . ",
         " . (int)($data['tail_promoted_count'] ?? 0) . ",
         " . (int)($data['tail_promoted_hit_count'] ?? 0) . ",
         " . (int)($data['calibration_enabled'] ?? 0) . ",
         " . (int)($data['calibration_active'] ?? 0) . ",
         " . (int)($data['shared_learning_enabled'] ?? 0) . ",
         " . (int)($data['pairwise_enabled'] ?? 0) . ",
         " . (int)($data['gibbs_enabled'] ?? 0) . ",
         " . (int)($data['live_tuning_enabled'] ?? 0) . ",
         " . $db->quote((string)($data['confidence_level'] ?? 'insufficient_data')) . ",
         " . $db->quote((string)($data['trend_bucket'] ?? 'unknown')) . ",
         " . $db->quote((string)$notesJson) . ",
         " . $db->quote($now) . ",
         " . $db->quote($now) . "
        )
        ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE
         `lottery_name`            = VALUES(`lottery_name`),
         `draw_date`               = VALUES(`draw_date`),
         `run_date`                = VALUES(`run_date`),
         `run_uuid`                = VALUES(`run_uuid`),
         `snapshot_run_uuid`       = VALUES(`snapshot_run_uuid`),
         `run_identity_key`        = VALUES(`run_identity_key`),
         `prediction_target_id`    = VALUES(`prediction_target_id`),
         `prediction_target_key`   = VALUES(`prediction_target_key`),
         `target_game_id`          = VALUES(`target_game_id`),
         `target_lottery_id`       = VALUES(`target_lottery_id`),
         `target_draw_date`        = VALUES(`target_draw_date`),
         `target_draw_at`          = VALUES(`target_draw_at`),
         `target_draw_session`     = VALUES(`target_draw_session`),
         `target_timezone_id`      = VALUES(`target_timezone_id`),
         `target_prediction_family`= VALUES(`target_prediction_family`),
         `target_prediction_type`  = VALUES(`target_prediction_type`),
         `actual_numbers_json`     = VALUES(`actual_numbers_json`),
         `predicted_ranking_json`  = VALUES(`predicted_ranking_json`),
         `avg_winning_rank`        = VALUES(`avg_winning_rank`),
         `best_winning_rank`       = VALUES(`best_winning_rank`),
         `worst_winning_rank`      = VALUES(`worst_winning_rank`),
         `median_winning_rank`     = VALUES(`median_winning_rank`),
         `weighted_quality_score`  = VALUES(`weighted_quality_score`),
         `top10_hits`              = VALUES(`top10_hits`),
         `top20_hits`              = VALUES(`top20_hits`),
         `top30_hits`              = VALUES(`top30_hits`),
         `top50_hits`              = VALUES(`top50_hits`),
         `missing_hits_count`      = VALUES(`missing_hits_count`),
         `combo_hit_summary_json`  = VALUES(`combo_hit_summary_json`),
         `tail_enabled`            = VALUES(`tail_enabled`),
         `tail_active`             = VALUES(`tail_active`),
         `tail_promoted_count`     = VALUES(`tail_promoted_count`),
         `tail_promoted_hit_count` = VALUES(`tail_promoted_hit_count`),
         `calibration_enabled`     = VALUES(`calibration_enabled`),
         `calibration_active`      = VALUES(`calibration_active`),
         `shared_learning_enabled` = VALUES(`shared_learning_enabled`),
         `pairwise_enabled`        = VALUES(`pairwise_enabled`),
         `gibbs_enabled`           = VALUES(`gibbs_enabled`),
         `live_tuning_enabled`     = VALUES(`live_tuning_enabled`),
         `confidence_level`        = VALUES(`confidence_level`),
         `trend_bucket`            = VALUES(`trend_bucket`),
         `notes_json`              = VALUES(`notes_json`),
         `updated_at`              = VALUES(`updated_at`)";
    try {
        $db->setQuery($sql);
        $db->execute();
        return true;
    } catch (Exception $e) {
        return false;
    }
}

/**
 * Migrate the skai_learning_performance table's unique index from the old
 * three-column key (user_id, run_id, source) to the corrected four-column key
 * (user_id, run_id, draw_date, source).
 *
 * WHY THIS IS NECESSARY:
 * The original key `uq_user_run_source` only covered (user_id, run_id, source).
 * This meant that when the same saved prediction run was evaluated against a new
 * draw date, the INSERT ... ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE silently overwrote the previous
 * performance record instead of appending a new one - breaking true historical
 * accumulation across multiple draw cycles.
 *
 * Adding `draw_date` to the key ensures each run produces exactly one record per
 * draw date, allowing the panel to show a genuine per-draw history.
 *
 * MIGRATION SAFETY:
 * - Reads INFORMATION_SCHEMA before any ALTER to avoid unnecessary writes.
 * - DROP and ADD are wrapped in separate try/catch blocks so a failure on one
 *   step does not abort the other.
 * - Existing rows are unaffected: with the old key there was at most one row per
 *   (run_id, source), so no (run_id, draw_date, source) duplicates can exist.
 * - Safe to call on every page load; both checks are read-only until a change
 *   is actually needed.
 *
 * @param  object $db  Joomla DB object
 * @return bool  true if migration was a no-op or succeeded, false on hard error
 */
function skaiMigratePerformanceTableKeys($db)
{
    $prefix  = $db->getPrefix();
    $rawName = $prefix . 'skai_learning_performance';

    // -- Check whether the OLD key still exists ----------------------------
    $oldKeyExists = false;
    try {
        $db->setQuery(
            "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM INFORMATION_SCHEMA.STATISTICS
              WHERE TABLE_SCHEMA = DATABASE()
                AND TABLE_NAME   = " . $db->quote($rawName) . "
                AND INDEX_NAME   = 'uq_user_run_source'"
        );
        $oldKeyExists = ((int)$db->loadResult() > 0);
    } catch (Exception $e) {
        return false;
    }

    // -- Check whether the NEW key already exists --------------------------
    $newKeyExists = false;
    try {
        $db->setQuery(
            "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM INFORMATION_SCHEMA.STATISTICS
              WHERE TABLE_SCHEMA = DATABASE()
                AND TABLE_NAME   = " . $db->quote($rawName) . "
                AND INDEX_NAME   = 'uq_user_run_draw_source'"
        );
        $newKeyExists = ((int)$db->loadResult() > 0);
    } catch (Exception $e) {
        return false;
    }

    // -- Drop the old key if present ---------------------------------------
    if ($oldKeyExists) {
        try {
            $db->setQuery("ALTER TABLE `{$rawName}` DROP INDEX `uq_user_run_source`");
            $db->execute();
        } catch (Exception $e) {
            // Ignore - may already have been removed by a parallel request
        }
    }

    // -- Add the new key if not yet present --------------------------------
    if (!$newKeyExists) {
        try {
            $db->setQuery(
                "ALTER TABLE `{$rawName}`
                  ADD UNIQUE KEY `uq_user_run_draw_source` (`user_id`,`run_id`,`draw_date`,`source`)"
            );
            $db->execute();
        } catch (Exception $e) {
            // Ignore - may already have been added by a parallel request
        }
    }

    return true;
}

/**
 * Load performance history for a user and optional lottery.
 * Returns records ordered by draw_date DESC (most recent first).
 *
 * @param  object $db
 * @param  int    $userId
 * @param  int    $lotteryId  0 = all lotteries
 * @param  int    $limit      Max records
 * @return array
 */
function skaiLoadPerformanceHistory($db, $userId, $lotteryId, $limit)
{
    $userId = (int) $userId;
    if ($userId <= 0) {
        return array();
    }

    $prefix  = $db->getPrefix();
    $tblName = '`' . $prefix . 'skai_learning_performance`';
    $limit   = max(1, min(500, (int)$limit));

    $where = '`user_id` = ' . $userId;
    if ((int)$lotteryId > 0) {
        $where .= ' AND `lottery_id` = ' . (int)$lotteryId;
    }
    $sql = "SELECT * FROM {$tblName} WHERE {$where} ORDER BY `draw_date` DESC, `run_id` DESC LIMIT {$limit}";
    try {
        $db->setQuery($sql);
        $rows = $db->loadAssocList();
        return is_array($rows) ? $rows : array();
    } catch (Exception $e) {
        return array();
    }
}

/**
 * Compute rolling trend analytics from a set of performance records.
 * Records must be sorted most-recent-first.
 *
 * Trend metric: avg_winning_rank (lower = better).
 * Improvement = current 10-run window avg rank is LOWER than prior 10-run window.
 * delta_10vs20 is positive when rank improved (lower number).
 *
 * @param  array $records  Performance records (most recent first)
 * @return array
 */
function skaiComputeTrend(array $records)
{
    // Only use records with a valid avg_winning_rank
    $scored = array();
    foreach ($records as $rec) {
        if (isset($rec['avg_winning_rank']) && $rec['avg_winning_rank'] !== null && $rec['avg_winning_rank'] !== '') {
            $scored[] = (float)$rec['avg_winning_rank'];
        }
    }

    $total = count($scored);

    if ($total < 5) {
        $confidence = 'insufficient_data';
    } elseif ($total < 15) {
        $confidence = 'early_trend';
    } elseif ($total < 35) {
        $confidence = 'moderate';
    } else {
        $confidence = 'strong';
    }

    if ($total === 0) {
        return array(
            'last10_avg'   => null,
            'last25_avg'   => null,
            'last50_avg'   => null,
            'lifetime_avg' => null,
            'sample_count' => 0,
            'delta_10vs20' => null,
            'trend_bucket' => 'unknown',
            'confidence'   => 'insufficient_data',
        );
    }

    $avgFn = function (array $slice) {
        if (empty($slice)) { return null; }
        return round(array_sum($slice) / count($slice), 2);
    };

    $last10   = $avgFn(array_slice($scored, 0, 10));
    $last25   = $avgFn(array_slice($scored, 0, 25));
    $last50   = $avgFn(array_slice($scored, 0, 50));
    $lifetime = $avgFn($scored);

    // Compare most recent 10 vs the 10 before that.
    // Positive delta_10vs20 = rank improved (current avg rank is lower = better).
    $delta       = null;
    $trendBucket = 'unknown';
    if ($total >= 10) {
        $currentWindow = $avgFn(array_slice($scored, 0, 10));
        $priorWindow   = $avgFn(array_slice($scored, 10, 10));
        if ($priorWindow !== null && $currentWindow !== null) {
            $delta = round($priorWindow - $currentWindow, 2);
            // 1.5 rank positions = minimum meaningful shift threshold.
            // Smaller differences are treated as 'flat' (within normal variance).
            $trendThreshold = 1.5;
            if ($delta > $trendThreshold) {
                $trendBucket = 'improving';
            } elseif ($delta < -$trendThreshold) {
                $trendBucket = 'declining';
            } else {
                $trendBucket = 'flat';
            }
        }
    }

    return array(
        'last10_avg'   => $last10,
        'last25_avg'   => $last25,
        'last50_avg'   => $last50,
        'lifetime_avg' => $lifetime,
        'sample_count' => $total,
        'delta_10vs20' => $delta,
        'trend_bucket' => $trendBucket,
        'confidence'   => $confidence,
    );
}

/**
 * Derive a user-friendly scorecard status from trend analytics.
 * Status determination logic is fully documented here:
 *
 * - confidence=insufficient_data              => 'Needs More Data'
 * - confidence=early_trend + improving bucket => 'Early Positive Trend'
 * - confidence=early_trend + declining bucket => 'Early Negative Trend'
 * - confidence=moderate/strong + improving    => 'Improving'
 * - confidence=moderate/strong + flat         => 'Stable'
 * - confidence=moderate/strong + declining    => 'Declining'
 * - unknown trend bucket                      => 'Calibrating'
 *
 * @param  array $trend  Result of skaiComputeTrend()
 * @return array { label, class, description }
 */
function skaiDeriveScorecard(array $trend)
{
    $conf   = (string)($trend['confidence']   ?? 'insufficient_data');
    $bucket = (string)($trend['trend_bucket'] ?? 'unknown');

    if ($conf === 'insufficient_data') {
        return array(
            'label'       => 'Needs More Data',
            'class'       => 'skai-lp-status--neutral',
            'description' => 'More validated draw results are needed to measure a reliable trend. Keep saving runs and checking back after each draw.',
        );
    }
    if ($bucket === 'improving') {
        if ($conf === 'early_trend') {
            return array(
                'label'       => 'Early Positive Trend',
                'class'       => 'skai-lp-status--good',
                'description' => 'Early evidence suggests predictions are improving. Continue monitoring as more draws are validated.',
            );
        }
        return array(
            'label'       => 'Improving',
            'class'       => 'skai-lp-status--good',
            'description' => 'Winning numbers are consistently appearing higher in SKAI\'s ranked predictions over recent draws.',
        );
    }
    if ($bucket === 'declining') {
        if ($conf === 'early_trend') {
            return array(
                'label'       => 'Early Negative Trend',
                'class'       => 'skai-lp-status--caution',
                'description' => 'Early data suggests prediction quality may be declining. Review your settings and method selection.',
            );
        }
        return array(
            'label'       => 'Declining',
            'class'       => 'skai-lp-status--bad',
            'description' => 'Average winning rank has increased over recent draws. Consider reviewing SKAI settings or method combinations.',
        );
    }
    if ($bucket === 'flat') {
        return array(
            'label'       => 'Stable',
            'class'       => 'skai-lp-status--neutral',
            'description' => 'Prediction quality is consistent. No significant improvement or decline detected in recent draws.',
        );
    }
    return array(
        'label'       => 'Calibrating',
        'class'       => 'skai-lp-status--neutral',
        'description' => 'Not enough completed draw cycles to calculate a performance trend yet.',
    );
}

/**
 * Determine a module's activity status from a flag count and sample size.
 * Called in the per-group panel to avoid redeclaring an inline function.
 *
 * Returns 'active' if the flag is set in > 50% of sampled runs,
 * 'building' if set in any runs, or 'inactive'.
 *
 * @param  int $count        Number of runs where the flag was set
 * @param  int $sampleCount  Total runs sampled
 * @return string
 */
function skaiLpModStatus($count, $sampleCount)
{
    if ($sampleCount <= 0 || $count <= 0) { return 'inactive'; }
    // 'active' = flag set in a strict majority (> 50%) of sampled runs
    $majorityThreshold = (int)floor($sampleCount / 2) + 1;
    return ($count >= $majorityThreshold) ? 'active' : 'building';
}

/**
 * Grade a single average-rank value into a performance tier.
 *
 * Grading scale (documented for auditability):
 *   1-5   -> Elite    (winning numbers appear in the very top predictions)
 *   6-10  -> Strong   (winning numbers near the top)
 *   11-20 -> Moderate (winning numbers in the middle range)
 *   21+   -> Weak     (winning numbers appear far down the list)
 *   null  -> Unknown
 *
 * @param  float|null $avgRank
 * @return array { grade, label, class }
 */
function skaiLpGrade($avgRank)
{
    if ($avgRank === null || $avgRank === '') {
        return array('grade' => 'unknown', 'label' => 'Unknown', 'class' => 'skai-lp-grade--unknown');
    }
    $r = (float)$avgRank;
    if ($r <= 5)  { return array('grade' => 'elite',    'label' => 'Elite',    'class' => 'skai-lp-grade--elite'); }
    if ($r <= 10) { return array('grade' => 'strong',   'label' => 'Strong',   'class' => 'skai-lp-grade--strong'); }
    if ($r <= 20) { return array('grade' => 'moderate', 'label' => 'Moderate', 'class' => 'skai-lp-grade--moderate'); }
    return             array('grade' => 'weak',     'label' => 'Weak',     'class' => 'skai-lp-grade--weak');
}

/**
 * Calculate performance consistency from an array of avg_rank values.
 *
 * Method: population standard deviation of avg_winning_rank values.
 * Lower standard deviation = more consistent / predictable performance.
 *
 * Classification thresholds (documented):
 *   stddev < 3   -> High Consistency   (runs produce similar results)
 *   stddev < 7   -> Moderate           (some run-to-run variation)
 *   stddev >= 7  -> Volatile           (unpredictable swings)
 *
 * @param  array $records  Performance records (most-recent-first), each with 'avg_winning_rank'
 * @return array { level, label, description, stddev }
 */
function skaiLpConsistency(array $records)
{
    $vals = array();
    foreach ($records as $rec) {
        if (isset($rec['avg_winning_rank']) && $rec['avg_winning_rank'] !== null && $rec['avg_winning_rank'] !== '') {
            $vals[] = (float)$rec['avg_winning_rank'];
        }
    }
    $n = count($vals);
    if ($n < 3) {
        return array(
            'level'       => 'unknown',
            'label'       => 'Not enough data',
            'description' => 'At least 3 scored runs are needed to measure consistency.',
            'stddev'      => null,
        );
    }
    $mean   = array_sum($vals) / $n;
    $sumSq  = 0.0;
    foreach ($vals as $v) { $sumSq += ($v - $mean) * ($v - $mean); }
    $stddev = round(sqrt($sumSq / $n), 2);

    // Consistency thresholds (named for auditability / easy tuning):
    //   High: stddev < 3 rank positions  (tight, reliable output)
    //   Moderate: stddev 3-7 rank positions (some run-to-run variation)
    //   Volatile: stddev >= 7 rank positions (unpredictable swings)
    $CONS_HIGH_THRESHOLD     = 3.0;
    $CONS_MODERATE_THRESHOLD = 7.0;

    if ($stddev < $CONS_HIGH_THRESHOLD) {
        return array(
            'level'       => 'high',
            'label'       => 'High Consistency',
            'description' => 'Results are reliably similar across runs. SKAI performs predictably.',
            'stddev'      => $stddev,
        );
    }
    if ($stddev < $CONS_MODERATE_THRESHOLD) {
        return array(
            'level'       => 'moderate',
            'label'       => 'Moderate Consistency',
            'description' => 'Some variation between runs, with occasional stronger or weaker results.',
            'stddev'      => $stddev,
        );
    }
    return array(
        'level'       => 'volatile',
        'label'       => 'Volatile',
        'description' => 'Performance varies significantly between runs. Results are unpredictable.',
        'stddev'      => $stddev,
    );
}

/**
 * Produce enriched trend intelligence: direction + strength labels.
 *
 * Direction: improving / declining / flat / unknown
 * Strength classification based on absolute delta (documented):
 *   |delta| < 1.5  -> no meaningful change (flat)
 *   |delta| 1.5-4  -> Slight
 *   |delta| 4-8    -> Moderate
 *   |delta| >= 8   -> Strong
 *
 * @param  float|null $delta  Positive = improving (rank decreased), negative = declining
 * @return array { direction, strength, label, arrow }
 */
function skaiLpTrendIntelligence($delta)
{
    if ($delta === null) {
        return array(
            'direction' => 'unknown',
            'strength'  => '',
            'label'     => 'Not enough data for a trend',
            'arrow'     => '-',
        );
    }
    $abs = abs((float)$delta);

    // Trend thresholds (named for auditability / easy tuning):
    //   Flat:     |delta| < 1.5  (within normal variance; no meaningful change)
    //   Slight:   |delta| 1.5-4  (noticeable but modest shift)
    //   Moderate: |delta| 4-8    (clear improvement or decline)
    //   Strong:   |delta| >= 8   (large rank shift across periods)
    $TREND_FLAT_THRESHOLD     = 1.5;
    $TREND_SLIGHT_THRESHOLD   = 4.0;
    $TREND_MODERATE_THRESHOLD = 8.0;

    if ($abs < $TREND_FLAT_THRESHOLD) {
        return array(
            'direction' => 'flat',
            'strength'  => 'minimal',
            'label'     => 'No significant change',
            'arrow'     => '-',
        );
    }

    // Strength tiers
    if ($abs < $TREND_SLIGHT_THRESHOLD)   { $strength = 'Slight'; }
    elseif ($abs < $TREND_MODERATE_THRESHOLD) { $strength = 'Moderate'; }
    else                                  { $strength = 'Strong'; }

    if ((float)$delta > 0) {
        return array(
            'direction' => 'improving',
            'strength'  => $strength,
            'label'     => $strength . ' Improvement',
            'arrow'     => 'Up',
        );
    }
    return array(
        'direction' => 'declining',
        'strength'  => $strength,
        'label'     => $strength . ' Decline',
        'arrow'     => 'Down',
    );
}

/**
 * Extract best-run opportunity metrics from the history records.
 * These metrics show the system's CEILING performance, not its average.
 *
 * @param  array $records  Performance records
 * @return array { best_avg_rank, best_single_rank, max_top10_hits, max_top20_hits,
 *                 best_weighted_score, best_run_date }
 */
function skaiLpBestRunStats(array $records)
{
    $bestAvgRank    = null;
    $bestSingleRank = null;
    $maxTop10       = 0;
    $maxTop20       = 0;
    $bestWScore     = null;
    $bestRunDate    = '';

    foreach ($records as $rec) {
        $avg = (isset($rec['avg_winning_rank']) && $rec['avg_winning_rank'] !== null && $rec['avg_winning_rank'] !== '')
            ? (float)$rec['avg_winning_rank'] : null;
        $best = (isset($rec['best_winning_rank']) && $rec['best_winning_rank'] !== null)
            ? (int)$rec['best_winning_rank'] : null;
        $t10  = (int)($rec['top10_hits'] ?? 0);
        $t20  = (int)($rec['top20_hits'] ?? 0);
        $ws   = (isset($rec['weighted_quality_score']) && $rec['weighted_quality_score'] !== null && $rec['weighted_quality_score'] !== '')
            ? (float)$rec['weighted_quality_score'] : null;

        // Best (lowest) average rank
        if ($avg !== null && ($bestAvgRank === null || $avg < $bestAvgRank)) {
            $bestAvgRank = $avg;
            $bestRunDate = (string)($rec['draw_date'] ?? '');
        }
        // Best (lowest) single number rank
        if ($best !== null && ($bestSingleRank === null || $best < $bestSingleRank)) {
            $bestSingleRank = $best;
        }
        // Best top-N hit counts
        if ($t10 > $maxTop10) { $maxTop10 = $t10; }
        if ($t20 > $maxTop20) { $maxTop20 = $t20; }
        // Best weighted score (higher = better)
        if ($ws !== null && ($bestWScore === null || $ws > $bestWScore)) {
            $bestWScore = $ws;
        }
    }

    return array(
        'best_avg_rank'    => ($bestAvgRank    !== null) ? round($bestAvgRank, 1) : null,
        'best_single_rank' => $bestSingleRank,
        'max_top10_hits'   => $maxTop10,
        'max_top20_hits'   => $maxTop20,
        'best_weighted'    => ($bestWScore !== null) ? round($bestWScore, 3) : null,
        'best_run_date'    => $bestRunDate,
    );
}

// ==========================================================================
// DUAL BEST-RUN MODEL - Scoring & Selection
// Separates two distinct concepts:
//   A. Best ranking setup example  - strongest ranking quality (lowest avg rank, etc.)
//   B. Best Hit Run     - strongest hit-oriented outcome (highest Top-20, etc.)
//   C. Recommended run  - composite recommendation under configurable strategy
// ==========================================================================

// Sentinel value for missing/null rank values.
// Treated as worst case (very high rank position) during scoring.
if (!defined('SKAI_LP_NULL_RANK_SENTINEL')) {
    define('SKAI_LP_NULL_RANK_SENTINEL', 9999.0);
}

/**
 * Safe [0..1] normalization helper.
 * When max == min (all values are equal), returns 1.0 so every record
 * is treated as "best possible" rather than being penalised.
 * If max < min (inverted range - should not happen in practice), also
 * returns 1.0 defensively rather than producing a negative result.
 * Clamps val to the [min..max] range before normalizing.
 *
 * @param  float $val
 * @param  float $min
 * @param  float $max
 * @return float  normalized value in [0..1]
 */
function skaiLpNormalize($val, $min, $max)
{
    // Guard: zero or inverted range -> treat as "all equal / best"
    if ((float)$max - (float)$min < 1.0e-10) { return 1.0; }
    $v = max((float)$min, min((float)$max, (float)$val));
    return ($v - (float)$min) / ((float)$max - (float)$min);
}

/**
 * Compute per-record composite scores for all records in the history array.
 *
 * Scoring formulas (all weights documented and sum to 1.00):
 *
 * SYSTEM QUALITY SCORE - measures ranking intelligence:
 *   sys = (norm_avg_rank_inv x 0.45)   // lower avg rank -> better
 *       + (norm_weighted    x 0.30)   // higher weighted score -> better
 *       + (norm_best_rank_inv x 0.15) // lower best rank -> better
 *       + (norm_missing_inv x 0.10)   // fewer missing -> better
 *
 * HIT OPPORTUNITY SCORE - measures playable hit-range outcome:
 *   hit = (norm_top20       x 0.45)   // more Top-20 hits -> better
 *       + (norm_top10       x 0.30)   // more Top-10 hits -> better
 *       + (norm_missing_inv x 0.15)   // fewer missing -> better
 *       + (norm_avg_rank_inv x 0.10)  // lower avg rank -> secondary tie-break
 *
 * BALANCED RECOMMENDATION SCORE:
 *   balanced = (sys x 0.60) + (hit x 0.40)
 *
 * "Inv" normalization: for "lower is better" metrics, normalize the
 * INVERTED distance from the maximum:
 *   norm_inv = (max - val) / (max - min)
 * This maps min_val -> 1.0 (best) and max_val -> 0.0 (worst).
 *
 * Null / missing values are treated as worst case:
 *   avg_rank and best_rank -> sentinel 9999 (very high = worst)
 *   weighted_quality_score -> 0.0 (no quality)
 *
 * Ties are resolved by the highest composite score; if scores are equal,
 * the first occurring record (most recent, since history is newest-first) wins.
 *
 * @param  array $records  Performance records (most-recent-first)
 * @return array  One entry per record: { sys, hit, balanced }
 */
function skaiLpComputeRunScores(array $records)
{
    if (empty($records)) { return array(); }

    $NULL_RANK = SKAI_LP_NULL_RANK_SENTINEL;  // Sentinel for missing rank values (see constant above)

    $avgRanks  = array();
    $wScores   = array();
    $bestRanks = array();
    $missings  = array();
    $top10s    = array();
    $top20s    = array();

    foreach ($records as $rec) {
        $avgRanks[]  = (isset($rec['avg_winning_rank']) && $rec['avg_winning_rank'] !== null && $rec['avg_winning_rank'] !== '')
            ? (float)$rec['avg_winning_rank'] : $NULL_RANK;
        $wScores[]   = (isset($rec['weighted_quality_score']) && $rec['weighted_quality_score'] !== null && $rec['weighted_quality_score'] !== '')
            ? (float)$rec['weighted_quality_score'] : 0.0;
        $bestRanks[] = (isset($rec['best_winning_rank']) && $rec['best_winning_rank'] !== null)
            ? (float)$rec['best_winning_rank'] : $NULL_RANK;
        $missings[]  = (int)($rec['missing_hits_count'] ?? 0);
        $top10s[]    = (int)($rec['top10_hits'] ?? 0);
        $top20s[]    = (int)($rec['top20_hits'] ?? 0);
    }

    // Pre-compute range boundaries for normalization
    $minAvg  = min($avgRanks);   $maxAvg  = max($avgRanks);
    $minWs   = min($wScores);    $maxWs   = max($wScores);
    $minBest = min($bestRanks);  $maxBest = max($bestRanks);
    $minMis  = min($missings);   $maxMis  = max($missings);
    $minT10  = min($top10s);     $maxT10  = max($top10s);
    $minT20  = min($top20s);     $maxT20  = max($top20s);

    $scores = array();
    $n = count($records);
    for ($i = 0; $i < $n; $i++) {
        // "Lower is better" -> normalize the inverted distance from max
        $normAvgInv  = skaiLpNormalize($maxAvg  - $avgRanks[$i],  0.0, $maxAvg  - $minAvg);
        $normBestInv = skaiLpNormalize($maxBest - $bestRanks[$i], 0.0, $maxBest - $minBest);
        $normMisInv  = skaiLpNormalize($maxMis  - $missings[$i],  0.0, $maxMis  - $minMis);
        // "Higher is better" -> standard normalization
        $normWs  = skaiLpNormalize($wScores[$i], $minWs,  $maxWs);
        $normT10 = skaiLpNormalize($top10s[$i],  $minT10, $maxT10);
        $normT20 = skaiLpNormalize($top20s[$i],  $minT20, $maxT20);

        // System Quality Score (weights total 1.00)
        $sys = ($normAvgInv  * 0.45)
             + ($normWs      * 0.30)
             + ($normBestInv * 0.15)
             + ($normMisInv  * 0.10);

        // Hit Opportunity Score (weights total 1.00)
        $hit = ($normT20    * 0.45)
             + ($normT10    * 0.30)
             + ($normMisInv * 0.15)
             + ($normAvgInv * 0.10);

        // Balanced Recommendation Score
        $balanced = ($sys * 0.60) + ($hit * 0.40);

        $scores[] = array(
            'sys'      => round($sys,      4),
            'hit'      => round($hit,      4),
            'balanced' => round($balanced, 4),
        );
    }

    return $scores;
}

/**
 * Internal helper: extract a clean display record from history + scores at index $idx.
 *
 * @param  array $records  Full history
 * @param  int   $idx      Record index
 * @return array
 */
/**
 * Build a compact, user-facing run-setting label from AI share used percent and
 * strategy family name.
 *
 * Examples:  "55% * Balanced Mix"   "72% * AI-Forward"   "28% * Skip Pattern Boost"
 *            "55%"  (family unknown)  "Balanced Mix"  (pct unknown)
 *            ""     (both unknown - legacy row)
 *
 * NOTE: AI percent is rounded to the nearest integer for display.
 * The strategy family thresholds (AI<=30, AI 31-69, AI>=70) match SKAI's canonical
 * blend classification. Sub-integer precision has no user-visible meaning.  Displaying
 * "55%" is always more readable than "55.4%" in a KPI label.
 *
 * @param  float|null  $aiPct       AI share used percent (0-100), or null if unknown
 * @param  string      $stratFamily Strategy family label, or '' if unknown
 * @return string
 */
function skaiLpFormatRunSettingLabel($aiPct, $stratFamily)
{
    $stratFamily = (string)$stratFamily;
    if ($aiPct !== null && $stratFamily !== '') {
        return (string)(int)round((float)$aiPct) . '% - ' . $stratFamily;
    }
    if ($aiPct !== null) {
        return (string)(int)round((float)$aiPct) . '%';
    }
    if ($stratFamily !== '') {
        return $stratFamily;
    }
    return '';
}

function skaiLpFormatTrainingStyleLabel($value)
{
    $value = trim((string)$value);
    if ($value === '') {
        return '';
    }
    $value = str_replace(array('_','-'), ' ', $value);
    return ucwords($value);
}


function skaiLpExtractRunRecord(array $records, $idx)
{
    $rec = $records[(int)$idx];
    $ts  = strtotime((string)($rec['draw_date'] ?? ''));

    // Decode run configuration persisted in notes_json.
    // Fields added when run_ai_pct / run_strategy_family were persisted at scoring time.
    // Graceful fallback for legacy rows that contain only [] or null.
    $notesArr = array();
    if (!empty($rec['notes_json'])) {
        $notesDecoded = json_decode((string)$rec['notes_json'], true);
        if (is_array($notesDecoded)) {
            $notesArr = $notesDecoded;
        }
    }
    $runAiPct       = (isset($notesArr['run_ai_pct']) && $notesArr['run_ai_pct'] !== null && $notesArr['run_ai_pct'] !== '')
                        ? round((float)$notesArr['run_ai_pct'], 1) : null;
    $runStratFamily = (isset($notesArr['run_strategy_family']) && (string)$notesArr['run_strategy_family'] !== '')
                        ? (string)$notesArr['run_strategy_family'] : '';

    // Build compact user-facing setting label via shared helper (avoids duplication)
    $runSettingLabel = skaiLpFormatRunSettingLabel($runAiPct, $runStratFamily);

    return array(
        'idx'                 => (int)$idx,
        'run_id'              => (int)($rec['run_id'] ?? 0),
        'label'               => $ts ? date('M j, Y', $ts) : (string)($rec['draw_date'] ?? ''),
        'avg_rank'            => (isset($rec['avg_winning_rank']) && $rec['avg_winning_rank'] !== null && $rec['avg_winning_rank'] !== '')
                                   ? round((float)$rec['avg_winning_rank'], 1) : null,
        'best_rank'           => ($rec['best_winning_rank'] !== null) ? (int)$rec['best_winning_rank'] : null,
        'weighted'            => (isset($rec['weighted_quality_score']) && $rec['weighted_quality_score'] !== null && $rec['weighted_quality_score'] !== '')
                                   ? round((float)$rec['weighted_quality_score'], 3) : null,
        'missing'             => (int)($rec['missing_hits_count'] ?? 0),
        'top10'               => (int)($rec['top10_hits'] ?? 0),
        'top20'               => (int)($rec['top20_hits'] ?? 0),
        // Run configuration fields (from notes_json, empty for legacy rows)
        'run_ai_pct'          => $runAiPct,
        'run_strategy_family' => $runStratFamily,
        'run_setting_label'   => $runSettingLabel,
    );
}

/**
 * Select the run with the strongest ranking quality (Best ranking setup example).
 *
 * Selection priority (via composite sys score):
 *   1. Lowest avg winning rank (weight 0.45)
 *   2. Highest weighted quality score (weight 0.30)
 *   3. Lowest best rank (weight 0.15)
 *   4. Lowest missing count (weight 0.10)
 * Ties: first record (most recent) wins.
 *
 * @param  array $records  Performance records
 * @return array|null  Run record + score, or null if no records
 */
function skaiLpSelectBestSystemRun(array $records)
{
    if (empty($records)) { return null; }

    $scores   = skaiLpComputeRunScores($records);
    $bestIdx  = 0;
    $bestScore = -1.0;
    foreach ($scores as $i => $s) {
        if ($s['sys'] > $bestScore) { $bestScore = $s['sys']; $bestIdx = $i; }
    }

    $result        = skaiLpExtractRunRecord($records, $bestIdx);
    $result['score'] = $bestScore;
    return $result;
}

/**
 * Select the run with the strongest hit-oriented outcome (Best Hit Run).
 *
 * Selection priority (via composite hit score):
 *   1. Highest Top-20 hits (weight 0.45)
 *   2. Highest Top-10 hits (weight 0.30)
 *   3. Lowest missing count (weight 0.15)
 *   4. Lowest avg winning rank (weight 0.10 - secondary tie-break)
 * Ties: first record (most recent) wins.
 *
 * @param  array $records  Performance records
 * @return array|null  Run record + score, or null if no records
 */
function skaiLpSelectBestHitRun(array $records)
{
    if (empty($records)) { return null; }

    $scores   = skaiLpComputeRunScores($records);
    $bestIdx  = 0;
    $bestScore = -1.0;
    foreach ($scores as $i => $s) {
        if ($s['hit'] > $bestScore) { $bestScore = $s['hit']; $bestIdx = $i; }
    }

    $result        = skaiLpExtractRunRecord($records, $bestIdx);
    $result['score'] = $bestScore;
    return $result;
}

/**
 * Select the recommended run for a given strategy.
 *
 * Supported modes:
 *   'balanced'      - 60% system quality + 40% hit opportunity (default)
 *   'hit_priority'  - pure hit opportunity score
 *   'rank_priority' - pure system quality score
 *
 * @param  array  $records  Performance records
 * @param  string $mode     Strategy mode (see above)
 * @return array|null  Run record + strategy metadata, or null if no records
 */
function skaiLpSelectRecommendedRun(array $records, $mode)
{
    if (empty($records)) { return null; }

    // Strategy -> score key + human labels
    $strategyMap = array(
        'balanced'      => array(
            'key'    => 'balanced',
            'label'  => 'Balanced Strategy',
            'reason' => 'This setting produced the strongest combined ranking quality and hit coverage in recent scored runs.',
        ),
        'hit_priority'  => array(
            'key'    => 'hit',
            'label'  => 'Hit Priority Strategy',
            'reason' => 'Maximizes hit-range outcomes - prioritizes Top-20 and Top-10 coverage.',
        ),
        'rank_priority' => array(
            'key'    => 'sys',
            'label'  => 'Rank Priority Strategy',
            'reason' => 'Focuses on overall ranking quality by prioritizing average rank and weighted score.',
        ),
    );
    $strategy  = $strategyMap[$mode] ?? $strategyMap['balanced'];
    $scoreKey  = $strategy['key'];

    $scores   = skaiLpComputeRunScores($records);
    $bestIdx  = 0;
    $bestScore = -1.0;
    foreach ($scores as $i => $s) {
        if ($s[$scoreKey] > $bestScore) { $bestScore = $s[$scoreKey]; $bestIdx = $i; }
    }

    $result                  = skaiLpExtractRunRecord($records, $bestIdx);
    $result['score']         = $bestScore;
    $result['mode']          = $mode;
    $result['strategy_label']= $strategy['label'];
    $result['reason']        = $strategy['reason'];
    return $result;
}

/**
 * Generate the "Why these results differ" explanation text for the dual-model panel.
 * Text is determined by whether the best system run and best hit run are the same record.
 *
 * @param  array|null $bestSys      Result of skaiLpSelectBestSystemRun()
 * @param  array|null $bestHit      Result of skaiLpSelectBestHitRun()
 * @param  int        $sampleCount  Total scored runs (for early-stage caveat)
 * @return string  HTML-safe plain-text explanation
 */
function skaiLpDualRunInsight($bestSys, $bestHit, $sampleCount)
{
    if ($bestSys === null || $bestHit === null) { return ''; }

    if ((int)$bestSys['idx'] === (int)$bestHit['idx']) {
        $text = 'The same run delivered both the best ranking quality and the strongest hit performance, '
              . 'indicating unusually strong alignment between system quality and playable outcome.';
    } else {
        $text = 'The strongest ranking-quality run differed from the strongest hit-oriented run. '
              . 'This shows that SKAI can rank winning numbers well overall while a different run '
              . 'may produce better short-range hit concentration - both results are valid and useful '
              . 'depending on what you are evaluating.';
    }

    if ((int)$sampleCount < 10) {
        $text .= ' Note: the current sample is still very early - these patterns will become more '
               . 'reliable as additional scored runs accumulate.';
    }

    return $text;
}

/**
 * Select the worst-performing run (highest average winning rank).
 * Consistent with the pattern used by skaiLpSelectBestSystemRun() and
 * skaiLpSelectBestHitRun() - avoids duplicating the null-safe loop inline.
 *
 * @param  array $records  Performance records
 * @return int|null  Array index of the worst run, or null if no scored records
 */
function skaiLpSelectWorstRun(array $records)
{
    $worstIdx = null;
    $worstVal = null;
    foreach ($records as $i => $rec) {
        $avg = (isset($rec['avg_winning_rank']) && $rec['avg_winning_rank'] !== null && $rec['avg_winning_rank'] !== '')
            ? (float)$rec['avg_winning_rank'] : null;
        if ($avg === null) { continue; }
        if ($worstVal === null || $avg > $worstVal) { $worstVal = $avg; $worstIdx = $i; }
    }
    return $worstIdx;
}

/**
 * Return a tiered confidence label with run-count context.
 *
 * Thresholds (documented):
 *   < 10 runs  -> Very Early Stage
 *   10-24      -> Early Stage
 *   25-49      -> Moderate Confidence
 *   50+        -> Strong Confidence
 *
 * @param  int $sampleCount
 * @return array { level, label, description }
 */
function skaiLpConfidenceLabel($sampleCount)
{
    $n = (int)$sampleCount;
    if ($n < 10) {
        return array(
            'level'       => 'very_early',
            'label'       => 'Very Early Stage',
            'description' => 'Results are based on fewer than 10 validated runs. Treat patterns as hints only.',
        );
    }
    if ($n < 25) {
        return array(
            'level'       => 'early',
            'label'       => 'Early Stage',
            'description' => 'Some patterns are emerging but the sample is still small. Observe a few more draw cycles before acting on trends.',
        );
    }
    if ($n < 50) {
        return array(
            'level'       => 'moderate',
            'label'       => 'Moderate Confidence',
            'description' => 'A meaningful sample is available. Trend direction and grade are likely reliable.',
        );
    }
    return array(
        'level'       => 'strong',
        'label'       => 'Strong Confidence',
        'description' => 'Large sample size. Performance metrics are statistically robust.',
    );
}

/**
 * Return an improved human-readable module status label and description.
 * Replaces "Not Detected" with context-appropriate language.
 *
 * @param  string $status  'active' | 'building' | 'inactive'
 * @return array { badge_class, badge_text, note }
 */
function skaiLpModStatusLabel($status)
{
    if ($status === 'active') {
        return array(
            'badge_class' => 'skai-lp-mod-badge--active',
            'badge_text'  => 'Active',
            'note'        => 'Detected in recent scored runs',
        );
    }
    if ($status === 'building') {
        return array(
            'badge_class' => 'skai-lp-mod-badge--building',
            'badge_text'  => 'Building',
            'note'        => 'Seen in some recent scored runs',
        );
    }
    return array(
        'badge_class' => 'skai-lp-mod-badge--inactive',
        'badge_text'  => 'Not recently detected',
        'note'        => 'Not detected in recent scored runs',
    );
}

/**
 * Hybrid module status for the latest accumulated draw context.
 * It combines:
 * 1) whether the latest draw context is using the engine now
 * 2) whether scored history supports that engine
 */
function skaiLpCurrentHybridStatus($activeNow, $historyCount, $sampleCount)
{
    $activeNow = ((int)$activeNow > 0);
    $historyCount = (int)$historyCount;
    $sampleCount = (int)$sampleCount;

    if ($activeNow) {
        if ($historyCount <= 0 || $sampleCount <= 0) {
            return array(
                'badge_class' => 'skai-lp-mod-badge--building',
                'badge_text'  => 'Active now',
                'note'        => 'Used in the latest draw context, but history is still early',
            );
        }
        $historyStatus = skaiLpModStatus($historyCount, $sampleCount);
        if ($historyStatus === 'active') {
            return array(
                'badge_class' => 'skai-lp-mod-badge--active',
                'badge_text'  => 'Active now',
                'note'        => 'Used in the latest draw context and supported by past results',
            );
        }
        return array(
            'badge_class' => 'skai-lp-mod-badge--building',
            'badge_text'  => 'Active now',
            'note'        => 'Used in the latest draw context and still building history',
        );
    }

    if ($historyCount > 0 && $sampleCount > 0) {
        $historyStatus = skaiLpModStatus($historyCount, $sampleCount);
        if ($historyStatus === 'active') {
            return array(
                'badge_class' => 'skai-lp-mod-badge--building',
                'badge_text'  => 'Used before',
                'note'        => 'Supported by past results, but not active in the latest draw context',
            );
        }
        return array(
            'badge_class' => 'skai-lp-mod-badge--inactive',
            'badge_text'  => 'Used before',
            'note'        => 'Seen in past results, but not active in the latest draw context',
        );
    }

    return array(
        'badge_class' => 'skai-lp-mod-badge--inactive',
        'badge_text'  => 'Not used now',
        'note'        => 'Not detected in the latest draw context or recent scored history',
    );
}

/**
 * Generate a dynamic "What this means" interpretation paragraph.
 * Combines grade, trend intelligence, and consistency into a single
 * plain-language insight for the user.
 *
 * DO NOT fake metrics - all conditions are based on real computed values.
 *
 * @param  array  $grade       Result of skaiLpGrade()
 * @param  array  $trend       Result of skaiLpTrendIntelligence()
 * @param  array  $consistency Result of skaiLpConsistency()
 * @param  array  $confidence  Result of skaiLpConfidenceLabel()
 * @return string  HTML-safe plain text paragraph
 */
function skaiLpInsight(array $grade, array $trend, array $consistency, array $confidence)
{
    // -- Grade phrase ----------------------------------------------
    $gradeMap = array(
        'elite'    => 'performing at an elite level',
        'strong'   => 'performing strongly',
        'moderate' => 'performing at a moderate level',
        'weak'     => 'producing weaker results than expected',
        'unknown'  => 'not yet rated',
    );
    $gradePhrase = $gradeMap[$grade['grade']] ?? 'performing at an unclassified level';

    // -- Trend phrase ----------------------------------------------
    $trendDir = $trend['direction'];
    if ($trendDir === 'improving') {
        $trendPhrase = 'with a ' . strtolower((string)$trend['strength']) . ' recent improvement in prediction quality';
    } elseif ($trendDir === 'declining') {
        $trendPhrase = 'but showing a ' . strtolower((string)$trend['strength']) . ' decline in recent runs';
    } elseif ($trendDir === 'flat') {
        $trendPhrase = 'with no significant recent change in quality';
    } else {
        $trendPhrase = 'with insufficient trend data';
    }

    // -- Consistency phrase ----------------------------------------
    $consMap = array(
        'high'     => 'Results are reliably consistent across runs.',
        'moderate' => 'Performance varies moderately between runs.',
        'volatile' => 'Results vary significantly between runs - some runs perform much better or worse than average.',
        'unknown'  => '',
    );
    $consPhrase = $consMap[$consistency['level']] ?? '';

    // -- Confidence caveat -----------------------------------------
    $confCaveat = '';
    if (in_array($confidence['level'], array('very_early', 'early'), true)) {
        $confCaveat = ' Note: the sample size is still small, so these trends may shift with more draws.';
    }

    $sentence = 'The system is currently ' . $gradePhrase . ', ' . $trendPhrase . '. '
              . ($consPhrase !== '' ? $consPhrase . ' ' : '')
              . $confCaveat;

    return trim($sentence);
}

// --------------------------------------------------------------
// Ensure SKAI Learning performance table exists (safe no-op if already created)
skaiEnsurePerformanceTable($db);
skaiEnsurePerformanceTableSchema($db);
// Migrate unique key to include draw_date, enabling true per-draw accumulation.
// On failure, log a warning so administrators can diagnose schema issues.
if (!skaiMigratePerformanceTableKeys($db)) {
    \Joomla\CMS\Log\Log::add(
        'SKAI Learning performance: unique key migration failed - check database permissions.',
        \Joomla\CMS\Log\Log::WARNING,
        'skai.learning'
    );
}

// --------------------------------------------------
// Numbers to Play payload endpoint for Wheel Builder import
// --------------------------------------------------
$__mleAction = strtolower(trim($app->input->getString('mle_action', '')));
// ============================================================
// ADVISORY BOARD FUNCTIONS
// ============================================================

/**
 * [[MLE_RANDOM_EDGE_UPGRADE]] Decode a JSON number payload safely.
 * Keeps this upgrade self-contained so older saved rows continue to work.
 */
function mleAdvisoryDecodeNumberPayload($raw)
{
    $raw = trim((string)$raw);
    if ($raw === '') {
        return array();
    }
    $decoded = json_decode($raw, true);
    if (json_last_error() !== JSON_ERROR_NONE) {
        return array();
    }
    $out = array();
    $walk = function ($value) use (&$walk, &$out) {
        if (is_array($value)) {
            foreach ($value as $v) {
                $walk($v);
            }
            return;
        }
        if (is_scalar($value)) {
            $text = trim((string)$value);
            if ($text === '') {
                return;
            }
            if (preg_match_all('/\d+/', $text, $m)) {
                foreach ($m[0] as $part) {
                    $n = (int)$part;
                    if ($n > 0 || $text === '0') {
                        $out[] = $n;
                    }
                }
            }
        }
    };
    $walk($decoded);
    return array_values($out);
}

/**
 * [[MLE_RANDOM_EDGE_UPGRADE]] Infer the candidate pool size used for random expectation.
 * The preferred source is predicted_ranking_json count. Fallbacks keep legacy rows scorable.
 */
function mleAdvisoryInferRankedPoolSize(array $row)
{
    $predicted = mleAdvisoryDecodeNumberPayload($row['predicted_ranking_json'] ?? '');
    $n = count($predicted);
    if ($n > 0) {
        return max(1, $n);
    }
    foreach (array('worst_winning_rank', 'best_winning_rank', 'median_winning_rank', 'avg_winning_rank') as $key) {
        if (isset($row[$key]) && is_numeric($row[$key]) && (float)$row[$key] > 0) {
            $n = max($n, (int)ceil((float)$row[$key]));
        }
    }
    if (!empty($row['top50_hits'])) { $n = max($n, 50); }
    elseif (!empty($row['top30_hits'])) { $n = max($n, 30); }
    elseif (!empty($row['top20_hits'])) { $n = max($n, 20); }
    elseif (!empty($row['top10_hits'])) { $n = max($n, 10); }
    return max(10, $n > 0 ? $n : 50);
}

/**
 * [[MLE_RANDOM_EDGE_UPGRADE]] Calculate one run's edge versus random rank expectation.
 * Rank lift > 1.00 means the observed average rank beat the random-rank baseline.
 */
function mleAdvisoryCalculateRandomEdgeForRow(array $row)
{
    $rankedPool = mleAdvisoryInferRankedPoolSize($row);
    $expectedRandomAvgRank = ($rankedPool + 1.0) / 2.0;

    $avgRank = (isset($row['avg_winning_rank']) && is_numeric($row['avg_winning_rank']) && (float)$row['avg_winning_rank'] > 0)
        ? (float)$row['avg_winning_rank'] : null;

    $actual = mleAdvisoryDecodeNumberPayload($row['actual_numbers_json'] ?? '');
    $actualCount = count($actual);
    $missing = max(0, (int)($row['missing_hits_count'] ?? 0));
    $found = 0;
    if ($actualCount > 0) {
        $found = max(0, $actualCount - $missing);
    } elseif ($avgRank !== null) {
        $found = max(1, (int)(($row['top50_hits'] ?? 0) ?: ($row['top30_hits'] ?? 0) ?: ($row['top20_hits'] ?? 0) ?: ($row['top10_hits'] ?? 1)));
        $actualCount = $found + $missing;
    }

    $missingPenaltyRank = $rankedPool + 1.0;
    $penalizedAvgRank = null;
    if ($avgRank !== null && ($found + $missing) > 0) {
        $penalizedAvgRank = (($avgRank * max(1, $found)) + ($missing * $missingPenaltyRank)) / max(1, ($found + $missing));
    }

    $top10 = (int)($row['top10_hits'] ?? 0);
    $top20 = (int)($row['top20_hits'] ?? 0);
    $expectedTop10 = ($actualCount > 0) ? ($actualCount * (min(10, $rankedPool) / $rankedPool)) : null;
    $expectedTop20 = ($actualCount > 0) ? ($actualCount * (min(20, $rankedPool) / $rankedPool)) : null;

    return array(
        'ranked_pool_size'         => $rankedPool,
        'expected_random_avg_rank' => round($expectedRandomAvgRank, 2),
        'avg_rank_lift'            => ($avgRank !== null && $avgRank > 0) ? round($expectedRandomAvgRank / $avgRank, 3) : 0.0,
        'penalized_avg_rank'       => ($penalizedAvgRank !== null) ? round($penalizedAvgRank, 2) : null,
        'penalized_rank_lift'      => ($penalizedAvgRank !== null && $penalizedAvgRank > 0) ? round($expectedRandomAvgRank / $penalizedAvgRank, 3) : 0.0,
        'top10_expected_hits'      => ($expectedTop10 !== null) ? round($expectedTop10, 3) : null,
        'top20_expected_hits'      => ($expectedTop20 !== null) ? round($expectedTop20, 3) : null,
        'top10_lift'               => ($expectedTop10 !== null && $expectedTop10 > 0) ? round($top10 / $expectedTop10, 3) : 0.0,
        'top20_lift'               => ($expectedTop20 !== null && $expectedTop20 > 0) ? round($top20 / $expectedTop20, 3) : 0.0,
        'actual_count'             => $actualCount,
        'found_count'              => $found,
        'missing_count'            => $missing,
    );
}

/**
 * [[MLE_RANDOM_EDGE_UPGRADE]] Aggregate edge metrics for a lottery or method.
 */
function mleAdvisoryAggregateRandomEdge(array $rows, $method = '')
{
    $method = mleAdvisorySourceToMethodKey($method);
    $sumAvgLift = 0.0;
    $sumPenLift = 0.0;
    $sumTop10Lift = 0.0;
    $sumTop20Lift = 0.0;
    $sumExpectedRank = 0.0;
    $sumPenRank = 0.0;
    $valid = 0;
    $positive = 0;
    $draws = array();
    foreach ($rows as $row) {
        if (!is_array($row)) { continue; }
        if ($method !== '' && $method !== 'unknown') {
            $rowMethod = mleAdvisorySourceToMethodKey($row['__method_key'] ?? $row['source'] ?? '');
            if ($rowMethod !== $method) { continue; }
        }
        $edge = mleAdvisoryCalculateRandomEdgeForRow($row);
        if (($edge['avg_rank_lift'] ?? 0) <= 0 && ($edge['penalized_rank_lift'] ?? 0) <= 0) { continue; }
        $valid++;
        $sumAvgLift += (float)$edge['avg_rank_lift'];
        $sumPenLift += (float)$edge['penalized_rank_lift'];
        $sumTop10Lift += (float)$edge['top10_lift'];
        $sumTop20Lift += (float)$edge['top20_lift'];
        $sumExpectedRank += (float)$edge['expected_random_avg_rank'];
        if ($edge['penalized_avg_rank'] !== null) { $sumPenRank += (float)$edge['penalized_avg_rank']; }
        if ((float)$edge['avg_rank_lift'] >= 1.05 && (float)$edge['penalized_rank_lift'] >= 1.00) { $positive++; }
        $dd = trim((string)($row['draw_date'] ?? ''));
        if ($dd !== '' && $dd !== '0000-00-00') { $draws[$dd] = 1; }
    }
    if ($valid <= 0) {
        return array(
            'sample_count' => 0,
            'validation_draws' => 0,
            'repeat_count' => 0,
            'avg_rank_lift' => 0.0,
            'penalized_rank_lift' => 0.0,
            'top10_lift' => 0.0,
            'top20_lift' => 0.0,
            'expected_random_avg_rank' => null,
            'penalized_avg_rank' => null,
            'edge_label' => 'Edge still measuring',
        );
    }
    $avgLift = round($sumAvgLift / $valid, 3);
    $penLift = round($sumPenLift / $valid, 3);
    $label = 'Edge still measuring';
    if ($avgLift >= 1.20 && $penLift >= 1.10) { $label = 'Strong edge vs random'; }
    elseif ($avgLift >= 1.15 && $penLift >= 1.05) { $label = 'Positive edge vs random'; }
    elseif ($avgLift >= 1.05 && $penLift >= 1.00) { $label = 'Early edge vs random'; }
    elseif ($avgLift >= 1.00) { $label = 'Near random baseline'; }
    else { $label = 'Below random baseline'; }

    return array(
        'sample_count' => $valid,
        'validation_draws' => count($draws),
        'repeat_count' => $positive,
        'avg_rank_lift' => $avgLift,
        'penalized_rank_lift' => $penLift,
        'top10_lift' => round($sumTop10Lift / $valid, 3),
        'top20_lift' => round($sumTop20Lift / $valid, 3),
        'expected_random_avg_rank' => round($sumExpectedRank / $valid, 2),
        'penalized_avg_rank' => round($sumPenRank / $valid, 2),
        'edge_label' => $label,
    );
}

/**
 * [[MLE_RANDOM_EDGE_UPGRADE]] Improved proof level for retention and statistical honesty.
 * Advancement now depends on sample size, repeated evidence, and lift over random expectation.
 */

// [[MLE_PRECISION_PROGRESS_MILESTONES_V14]]
// Centralized evidence ladder. A 9-run batch counts as multiple scored runs,
// but only one completed draw cycle. Stronger narrowing stages require BOTH
// completed draw-cycle support and scored-run support.
function mleAdvisoryPrecisionMilestoneDefinitions()
{
    return array(
        array('key'=>'learning_stage','label'=>'Learning Stage','draws'=>0,'runs'=>0,'clusters'=>0,'short'=>'Collecting evidence'),
        array('key'=>'early_direction','label'=>'Early Direction','draws'=>3,'runs'=>18,'clusters'=>0,'short'=>'Possible signal forming'),
        array('key'=>'exploratory_batch','label'=>'Exploratory Precision Batch','draws'=>5,'runs'=>45,'clusters'=>2,'short'=>'First controlled test after regular batch evidence'),
        array('key'=>'narrowing_signal','label'=>'Narrowing Signal','draws'=>6,'runs'=>45,'clusters'=>3,'short'=>'Repeated direction forming'),
        array('key'=>'validated_focus_range','label'=>'Validated Focus Range','draws'=>12,'runs'=>75,'clusters'=>4,'short'=>'Strong repeated evidence'),
        array('key'=>'precision_focus_range','label'=>'Precision Focus Range','draws'=>20,'runs'=>120,'clusters'=>5,'short'=>'Strongest disciplined range')
    );
}

function mleAdvisoryResolvePrecisionMilestoneStatus($currentKey, $milestoneKey)
{
    $order = array('learning_stage'=>0,'early_direction'=>1,'exploratory_batch'=>2,'narrowing_signal'=>3,'validated_focus_range'=>4,'precision_focus_range'=>5);
    $cur = isset($order[$currentKey]) ? (int)$order[$currentKey] : 0;
    $idx = isset($order[$milestoneKey]) ? (int)$order[$milestoneKey] : 0;
    if ($idx < $cur) { return 'complete'; }
    if ($idx === $cur) { return 'current'; }
    if ($idx === $cur + 1) { return 'next'; }
    return 'future';
}

function mleAdvisoryBuildPrecisionWaitingText($currentKey, $drawCount, $runCount, $clusterSupport)
{
    $defs = mleAdvisoryPrecisionMilestoneDefinitions();
    $order = array('learning_stage'=>0,'early_direction'=>1,'exploratory_batch'=>2,'narrowing_signal'=>3,'validated_focus_range'=>4,'precision_focus_range'=>5);
    $cur = isset($order[$currentKey]) ? (int)$order[$currentKey] : 0;
    if ($cur >= 5) {
        return 'Precision Focus Range is active. Keep validating future completed draws before treating this as a long-term baseline.';
    }
    $next = $defs[$cur + 1];
    $need = array();
    $moreDraws = max(0, (int)$next['draws'] - (int)$drawCount);
    $moreRuns = max(0, (int)$next['runs'] - (int)$runCount);
    $moreClusters = max(0, (int)$next['clusters'] - (int)$clusterSupport);
    if ($moreDraws > 0) { $need[] = $moreDraws . ' more completed draw cycle' . ($moreDraws === 1 ? '' : 's'); }
    if ($moreRuns > 0) { $need[] = $moreRuns . ' more scored run' . ($moreRuns === 1 ? '' : 's'); }
    if ($moreClusters > 0) { $need[] = $moreClusters . ' more repeated setting cluster' . ($moreClusters === 1 ? '' : 's'); }
    if (empty($need)) {
        return 'The next milestone is ready once the latest saved evidence is scored and reviewed.';
    }
    return 'Waiting for ' . implode(', ', $need) . ' to reach ' . $next['label'] . '.';
}

function mleAdvisoryGetProofLevelLabel($completedDraws, $totalScoredRuns, $topMethodPct = 0.0, $runnerUpPct = 0.0, array $edgeMetrics = array()) {
    $completedDraws  = (int)$completedDraws;
    $totalScoredRuns = (int)$totalScoredRuns;
    $topMethodPct    = (float)$topMethodPct;
    $runnerUpPct     = (float)$runnerUpPct;

    $avgRankLift       = isset($edgeMetrics['avg_rank_lift']) ? (float)$edgeMetrics['avg_rank_lift'] : 0.0;
    $penalizedRankLift = isset($edgeMetrics['penalized_rank_lift']) ? (float)$edgeMetrics['penalized_rank_lift'] : 0.0;
    $validationDraws   = isset($edgeMetrics['validation_draws']) ? (int)$edgeMetrics['validation_draws'] : $completedDraws;
    $repeatCount       = isset($edgeMetrics['repeat_count']) ? (int)$edgeMetrics['repeat_count'] : 0;
    $rangeWidth        = isset($edgeMetrics['range_width']) ? (float)$edgeMetrics['range_width'] : 100.0;
    $trendBucket       = strtolower(trim((string)($edgeMetrics['trend_bucket'] ?? 'unknown')));

    $methodGap = abs($topMethodPct - $runnerUpPct);
    $closeRace = ($topMethodPct > 0.0 && $runnerUpPct > 0.0 && $methodGap < 8.0);
    $trendHealthy = in_array($trendBucket, array('improving','flat','unknown',''), true);
    $hasBasicEdge = ($avgRankLift >= 1.05 && $penalizedRankLift >= 1.00);
    $hasGoodEdge  = ($avgRankLift >= 1.15 && $penalizedRankLift >= 1.05);
    $hasEliteEdge = ($avgRankLift >= 1.20 && $penalizedRankLift >= 1.10);

    if ($completedDraws < 3 || $totalScoredRuns < 18) {
        return array('level' => 'learning_stage', 'label' => 'Learning Stage',
            'description' => 'LottoExpert is collecting completed draw-cycle evidence. A batch can add many scored runs, but each target date still counts as one completed draw cycle.');
    }

    if ($closeRace && $completedDraws < 12) {
        return array('level' => 'close_race', 'label' => 'Close Race',
            'description' => 'Two methods are close. Keep both in testing until one shows a clearer edge across more completed draw cycles.');
    }

    if ($completedDraws >= 20 && $totalScoredRuns >= 120 && $validationDraws >= 20 && $hasEliteEdge && $rangeWidth <= 15.0 && $repeatCount >= 5 && $trendHealthy) {
        return array('level' => 'precision_focus_range', 'label' => 'Precision Focus Range',
            'description' => 'This is LottoExpert\'s strongest current disciplined range, supported across many completed draw cycles and repeated scored runs. It is not a guarantee.');
    }

    if ($completedDraws >= 12 && $totalScoredRuns >= 75 && $validationDraws >= 12 && $hasGoodEdge && $rangeWidth <= 20.0 && $repeatCount >= 4 && $trendHealthy) {
        return array('level' => 'validated_focus_range', 'label' => 'Validated Focus Range',
            'description' => 'This range has repeated support across completed draw cycles and positive lift versus random expectation. Use it as a disciplined testing range.');
    }

    if ($completedDraws >= 6 && $totalScoredRuns >= 45 && $hasGoodEdge && $repeatCount >= 3 && $trendHealthy) {
        return array('level' => 'narrowing_signal', 'label' => 'Narrowing Signal',
            'description' => 'Repeated completed-draw evidence is starting to favor a tighter strategy range. Keep future tests controlled.');
    }

    if ($completedDraws >= 5 && $totalScoredRuns >= 45 && $hasBasicEdge && $repeatCount >= 2) {
        return array('level' => 'exploratory_batch', 'label' => 'Exploratory Precision Batch',
            'description' => 'Enough regular batch evidence has built up to test a controlled 9-run Precision Focus batch. This is a structured test, not a conclusion.');
    }

    if ($completedDraws >= 3 && $totalScoredRuns >= 18 && ($avgRankLift >= 1.00 || $topMethodPct > $runnerUpPct)) {
        return array('level' => 'early_direction', 'label' => 'Early Direction',
            'description' => 'A possible direction is forming. LottoExpert will wait for more completed draw cycles before stronger narrowing guidance.');
    }

    return array('level' => 'no_clear_edge', 'label' => 'No Clear Edge Yet',
        'description' => 'Current scored results do not yet show a reliable edge over the baseline. Keep saving, scoring, and comparing after completed draws.');
}

function mleAdvisoryMethodLabel($method) {
    $raw = strtolower(trim((string)$method));
    $method = function_exists('mleAdvisorySourceToMethodKey') ? mleAdvisorySourceToMethodKey($raw) : $raw;
    $map = array(
        'skai' => 'SKAI', 'skai_prediction' => 'SKAI', 'skai_blend' => 'SKAI',
        'ai' => 'Neural AI', 'ai_prediction' => 'Neural AI', 'neural' => 'Neural AI',
        'mcmc' => 'MCMC', 'mcmc_prediction' => 'MCMC',
        'skip' => 'Skip/Hit', 'skip_hit' => 'Skip/Hit', 'skiphit' => 'Skip/Hit',
        'frequency' => 'Frequency', 'heatmap' => 'Frequency',
    );
    return isset($map[$method]) ? $map[$method] : ($method !== '' ? ucfirst($method) : 'Unknown');
}


/**
 * [[MLE_METHOD_AWARE_LEARNING_V25]] Method role model.
 * SKAI remains the flagship engine, while MCMC, Neural AI, Skip/Hit, and
 * Frequency/Heatmap become learning tracks or supporting baseline signals.
 */
function mleAdvisoryMethodRoleInfo($method) {
    $method = mleAdvisorySourceToMethodKey($method);
    $map = array(
        'skai' => array(
            'role' => 'Flagship Engine',
            'track' => 'SKAI Precision Track',
            'short_track' => 'SKAI Track',
            'badge' => 'flagship',
            'learns' => 'blend strength, history window, diversity, temperature, and controlled Precision Focus batches',
            'positioning' => 'SKAI is LottoExpert\'s most advanced engine. Use it as the primary precision workflow when its scored evidence is leading or when you want the most complete controlled-batch system.',
            'next_if_building' => 'Run regular SKAI batches and save them so LottoExpert can compare results across completed draw cycles.',
            'next_if_ready' => 'Create or run the SKAI Precision Focus batch when the card says the evidence is ready.',
            'focus_label' => 'SKAI Precision Focus Batch',
            'can_batch_now' => true,
        ),
        'mcmc' => array(
            'role' => 'Sampling Track',
            'track' => 'MCMC Sampling Track',
            'short_track' => 'MCMC Track',
            'badge' => 'sampling',
            'learns' => 'sampling depth, walks, burn-in, smoothing, decay, coverage spread, and repeated sampling profiles',
            'positioning' => 'MCMC is a serious sampling method. For MCMC-focused users, LottoExpert should learn which sampling profile repeats in stronger scored runs instead of forcing a SKAI batch.',
            'next_if_building' => 'Keep running MCMC saves for this lottery and score them after each draw so LottoExpert can find a stable sampling profile.',
            'next_if_ready' => 'Use the repeated MCMC settings as the control and test one sampling setting at a time. Full MCMC focus-batch handoff will be added on the MCMC page next.',
            'focus_label' => 'MCMC Sampling Focus Test',
            'can_batch_now' => false,
        ),
        'ai' => array(
            'role' => 'Classic AI Track',
            'track' => 'Neural AI Track',
            'short_track' => 'AI Track',
            'badge' => 'classic-ai',
            'learns' => 'training cycles, recency focus, overthinking protection, pattern depth, and repeated neural settings',
            'positioning' => 'Neural AI remains useful as a classic AI comparison track. LottoExpert can learn from it when users save and score Neural AI runs consistently.',
            'next_if_building' => 'Keep saving Neural AI runs for this lottery and score them after completed draws before treating the track as narrowed.',
            'next_if_ready' => 'Use the repeated Neural AI settings as the control and test one model setting at a time. Full Neural AI focus-batch handoff will be added on the Neural AI page next.',
            'focus_label' => 'Neural AI Focus Test',
            'can_batch_now' => false,
        ),
        'skip_hit' => array(
            'role' => 'Timing Signal',
            'track' => 'Skip/Hit Timing Track',
            'short_track' => 'Skip/Hit Track',
            'badge' => 'timing',
            'learns' => 'past draws checked, skip timing weight, history weight, and frequency weight when those settings are saved',
            'positioning' => 'Skip/Hit is best treated as a timing signal and comparison layer. It can support controlled weight testing when saved settings are available.',
            'next_if_building' => 'Keep saving Skip/Hit runs and compare whether they align with SKAI, MCMC, or Neural AI across completed draws.',
            'next_if_ready' => 'Use Skip/Hit as a timing-control test. Change only one timing weight at a time and score after the next draw.',
            'focus_label' => 'Skip/Hit Timing Weight Test',
            'can_batch_now' => false,
        ),
        'frequency' => array(
            'role' => 'Frequency Baseline',
            'track' => 'Manual Frequency Baseline',
            'short_track' => 'Frequency Baseline',
            'badge' => 'baseline',
            'learns' => 'manual frequency alignment and comparison against tunable engines',
            'positioning' => 'Frequency and Heatmap saves are valuable baselines, but they are mostly manual and should not be treated as automated narrowing engines unless tunable settings are saved.',
            'next_if_building' => 'Use Frequency Map as a baseline reference and compare future SKAI, MCMC, or Neural AI runs against it.',
            'next_if_ready' => 'Use the Frequency Map result as a comparison baseline. Run a tunable method if you want an automated focus test.',
            'focus_label' => 'Frequency Baseline Review',
            'can_batch_now' => false,
        ),
        'heatmap' => array(
            'role' => 'Frequency Baseline',
            'track' => 'Manual Frequency Baseline',
            'short_track' => 'Frequency Baseline',
            'badge' => 'baseline',
            'learns' => 'manual frequency alignment and comparison against tunable engines',
            'positioning' => 'Frequency and Heatmap saves are valuable baselines, but they are mostly manual and should not be treated as automated narrowing engines unless tunable settings are saved.',
            'next_if_building' => 'Use Frequency Map as a baseline reference and compare future SKAI, MCMC, or Neural AI runs against it.',
            'next_if_ready' => 'Use the Frequency Map result as a comparison baseline. Run a tunable method if you want an automated focus test.',
            'focus_label' => 'Frequency Baseline Review',
            'can_batch_now' => false,
        ),
    );
    if (!isset($map[$method])) {
        return array(
            'role' => 'Comparison Signal',
            'track' => 'Comparison Track',
            'short_track' => 'Comparison',
            'badge' => 'comparison',
            'learns' => 'saved-run performance compared against completed draw results',
            'positioning' => 'This method can be evaluated as a comparison signal when runs are saved and scored.',
            'next_if_building' => 'Keep saving and scoring runs for this method before using it for narrowing.',
            'next_if_ready' => 'Use the strongest repeated settings as a controlled comparison test.',
